use_when: A candidate object can be generated as code and scored cheaply, exactly, and repeatedly.
mechanism: Maintain a diverse archive of executable candidates; have models mutate programs, run a deterministic evaluator, retain improvements and informative novelties, and periodically recombine distinct lineages.
first_discriminator: Implement a tiny evaluator and verify that it ranks known good, bad, and adversarial controls correctly before spending on generation.
failure_modes: proxy score diverges from the mathematical objective
archive collapses to one lineage
floating-point or timeout artifacts look like improvements

use_when: The space is conceptual and several hypotheses or mechanisms can be compared by explicit criteria.
mechanism: Generate initially independent hypotheses, subject them to reflection and pairwise ranking, evolve the survivors, test proximity to established facts, then produce a synthesis that redirects the portfolio.
first_discriminator: Define a rubric with novelty, falsifiability, source consistency, cheapest test, and theorem-strength-blocker penalties; rank a small independent batch.
failure_modes: agents converge through shared wording rather than evidence
ranking rewards fluency
meta-review launders a repeated assumption

use_when: The target or useful supporting lemmas exist in Lean, or a narrow formalization is itself a contribution.
mechanism: Translate the exact statement, retrieve nearby declarations and premises from the repository, attempt short proof states programmatically, and preserve compilable partial lemmas as curriculum for later epochs.
first_discriminator: Create the minimal compiling statement and retrieve ten semantically and structurally nearby declarations; test whether one closes or materially simplifies a proof state.
failure_modes: formal statement is not equivalent to the source
premise search memorizes repository artifacts
a sorry/admit survives unnoticed

use_when: One explicit object refutes the universal claim or establishes a finite existence claim.
mechanism: Derive necessary constraints, enumerate canonical representatives, use exact arithmetic, minimize any witness, and emit a small independent certificate/checker.
first_discriminator: Check known controls and exhaust the smallest nontrivial parameter shell under two independent encodings.
failure_modes: symmetry breaking removes real witnesses
numeric tolerances create false witnesses
search scope is reported as universal

use_when: A finite nonexistence or lower-bound claim has an exact CNF encoding that is too large for one solver run but can be partitioned by explicit, disjoint branching assumptions.
mechanism: Generate a deterministic, symmetry-normalized CNF; split it into a fixed prefix tree of assumption cubes; retain every cube, its status, and its parent relation as the resumable search archive. For SAT leaves, independently check the decoded witness. For UNSAT leaves, retain an LRAT/DRAT certificate and verify it independently. Accept a global nonexistence result only when a separately checked cover certificate proves that the retained cubes exhaust the normalized search space and every leaf has a valid refutation; prioritize further compute only on uncovered or uncertified cubes.
first_discriminator: On a toy instance with a known answer, create at most 16 cubes and require: each cube is satisfiable or has a checked proof, pairwise cube disjointness holds syntactically, and the CNF plus the negation of their disjunction is independently UNSAT.
experiment_template: erdos-835 -> hypothesis: a selected small Johnson-graph coloring instance has no k-coloring after fixing a canonical vertex/color normalization -> deterministic Johnson-CNF generator; split on the first normalized color variables; run a proof-logging SAT solver per cube and verify each LRAT/DRAT leaf with an independent checker (or Lean LRAT importer) -> decisive signal: either a decoded coloring passes an independent edge checker, or all cubes plus a checked cover proof establish UNSAT -> stop condition: stop after 64 cubes or when every generated cube has a verified SAT witness or UNSAT certificate; report only the covered prefix if any cube remains uncertified
failure_modes: The symmetry normalization is unsound and excludes valid colorings.
A cube cover has a gap or overlap, so refuted leaves do not establish the global claim.
Proof logs certify the emitted CNF but the generator-to-graph correspondence is wrong.
Certificate storage or checking cost exceeds the bounded-epoch budget, making the partition too fine.

use_when: Small exact data may expose residue classes, parametric identities, invariants, or algebraic factorization patterns.
mechanism: Generate exact examples, infer candidate partitions or identities, symbolically verify each proposed family, and isolate the finite residue set left uncovered.
first_discriminator: Fit the simplest candidate family on half the data and require exact out-of-sample validation on the other half plus boundary cases.
failure_modes: interpolation is mistaken for explanation
residue classes overlap but do not cover
division-by-zero or positivity conditions are lost

use_when: A route claims progress or a candidate result and needs a genuinely independent attack.
mechanism: Give a skeptic only the source statement and claimed artifact, require reconstruction with a different representation, and log the first irreducible discrepancy rather than asking for agreement.
first_discriminator: Reproduce the smallest claimed lemma or witness from the source and certificate without reading the proposing agent's narrative.
failure_modes: reviewer inherits the same prompt assumptions
checker shares implementation code
absence of a found flaw is reported as proof
